programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC: Volume 1
programme.desc: Trevor Nelson presents the first volume of a celebration of some of the very best in black British music from over the decades, featuring 40 tantalising tracks.

programme.title: Stormzy: Live at Glastonbury 2019
programme.desc: The mega-star rapper takes the crown in a historic Glasto performance. Contains very strong language.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Highlights from the first day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey is drawn deeper into Simon's insidious plan, with devastating consequences. Contains strong language, graphic violence and upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa tries to find a way of getting Stephen to talk to her. Contains strong language and some upsetting scenes.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: We're in the Highlands and Islands of Scotland, where part-time mermaid Katrina is leading the group on a hike around Loch Long. But will her watery walk win her the prize.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith head to the National Forest in the Midlands as it reaches a milestone moment: the planting of its 10-millionth tree.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Landward explores depopulation, one of the biggest issues facing Scotland's fragile rural communities. The team meets the people battling to reverse the trend.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum Clunie kicks off his season at Beechgrove, demonstrating what can be grown from a container in just one season, while Brian looks at how to keep lawns looking great.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 2 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Andi Oliver's Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi goes to Cornwall to help a local chef put on a feast to thank NHS staff. Her idea of holding a feast for 80 people at a long table on a beach is tricky, but will it be amazing.

programme.title: Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved TV chefs. Ainsley picks dishes influenced by exciting world cuisine. The menu includes tapas from James Martin.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 2 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds. Items range from some folk art Scandinavian nutcrackers to a Napoleonic prisoner of war handcrafted box.

programme.title: Artemis: To the Moon and Back
programme.desc: The inside story of the Artemis II mission - filmed over three and a half years at NASA. How did four astronauts travel to the moon, going deeper into space than ever before.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Andrew is singled out by His Royal Highness at a farmers' market, Emma and Rob's first farm wedding and calving time collide, and Callum and Zara host historic sheepdog trials.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Police must determine if a haul of 3D-printed rifles are working, lethal guns. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: The King's Speech
programme.desc: The true story of a king who tried to overcome his stutter so he could address the nation. Contains strong language.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Highlights from the second day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of debate from Cardiff. On the panel are Huw Irranca-Davies, Rhun ap Iorwerth, Dan Thomas, Darren Millar, Jane Dodds and Anthony Slaughter.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa and Stephen navigate their new pregnancy. Dave tries to connect with his son. Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Yorkshire to help the Mann-Monro family, a blended household of six that has been overwhelmed by years of clutter and emotional memories.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts David Harper and Irita Marriott chase two high-energy teams around a packed antiques fair in the Scottish Borders town of Kelso.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden lives of foxes, hedgehogs, blue tits and frogs, each making a life for themselves in a small urban garden in Bristol.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Arsenal travel to Manchester City in a game that could be crucial in deciding this season's Premier League title race. Elsewhere, Everton face Liverpool at their new Hill Dickinson Stadium.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Alistair McGowan takes a trip through the BBC archives to look at the lives and careers of Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins, Jimmy 'Whirlwind' White and Ronnie 'the Rocket' O'Sullivan.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley test their general knowledge skills on this week's House of Games.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael is in Kagoshima, a city which sits in the shadow of Japan's most active volcano.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world.

programme.title: Mastermind Final
programme.desc: Clive Myrie asks the questions in the grand final. Subjects are Dame Julie Andrews, The Films of Danny Boyle, Beatrix Potter, Robert Burns, Notre-Dame Cathedral and Jim Lovell.
